# The Bronze Age Collapse

The Bronze Age collapse was a rapid breakdown of several advanced civilisations
around the eastern Mediterranean roughly 3,200 years ago. Within a few decades,
palaces were destroyed, cities were abandoned, long-distance trade unravelled, and
writing systems fell out of use across a wide region that had been densely connected.

## Possible causes

Historians debate the causes and suspect several reinforcing factors rather than a
single one: drought and famine, earthquakes, internal revolt, and raids by groups
recorded as the Sea Peoples. Because bronze depended on tin carried over long trade
routes, the disruption of trade may have been especially damaging. The collapse was
followed by a poorly documented period sometimes called a dark age.
